title: Procedures for Inspection of Veterinary Facilities Regulations
title_en: Procedures for Inspection of Veterinary Facilities Regulations

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Procedures for inspection of Veterinary Facilities Regulations.

provisions.title: Appiication
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to all registered Veterinary Facilities in Tanzania Mainland.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: in these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Veterinary Act, 2003; “ applicant ” means an individual who files an application to be registered to practice veterinary medicine; “ certificate ” means a certificate of registration issued to a veterinarian, veterinary specialist or veterinary facility , to an enrolled paraprofessional and enlisted paraprofessional assistant, or any other certificate issued pursuant to the Act ; “ council ” means as defined under Section 2 of the Act ; “ Deputy Registrar ” means as defined under section 8 of the Act ; “ facility ” means as defined in Section 2 of the Act ; “ inspection Plan ” means a plan to establish procedures to be followed for selection, conduct and reporting of inspections of veterinary facilities; “ inspector ” means as defined under Section 11 of the Act ; “ Minister ” means as defined under Section 2 of the Act ; “ register ” means as defined under Section 2 of the Act . Part II – Plan and conduct of inspections

provisions.title: Inspection plan
provisions.content: To obtain compliance with the regulations governing the practice of veterinary medicine, there shall be an inspection plan that establishes the procedures to be followed by the appointed Inspector and shall include but not limited to: (a) conduct of Inspection; (b) procedures and types of Inspections; (c) type of veterinary facilities subject to inspection; (d) inspection frequencies; (e) facility Inspection costs; (f) scrutinise and compile applications for enrolment and enlistment; (g) reporting of inspections deficiencies and evaluation of veterinary facilities to the Council.

provisions.title: Inspectors responsibilities
provisions.content: (1) The Inspector has legal responsibility not to disclose the facts learned during the course of inspection of facilities; (2) The Inspector shall observe an accurate report of facts during the course of inspection and investigations. (3) The Inspector shall have a strict code of ethics with respect to the duties stipulated in section 11 (2) of the Act .

provisions.title: Conduct of inspector
provisions.content: The conduct of Inspector during inspection visits to veterinary facilities shall be as follows:— (a) to contact the Veterinarian in charge of the veterinary facility by approaching him in dignified, authoritative and cordial manner; (b) to present credintials using Council’s identity cards, and explain the purpose of the visit; (c) to use diplomacy, tact and persuasiveness to acquire necessary information and details; (d) to inspect for the items listed on the inspection report form for that category of veterinary facilities; (e) do not become argumentative over any issue with no satisfactory answer; (f) do not answer questions relating to interpretation of Laws, regulations or policy issues, direct all such questions to the Council; (g) upon completion of the inspection, meet the veterinarian in charge to discuss the findings but adopt a courteous attitude in calling the attention to the violating practices or conditions observed at the time of inspection and on sport minor suggestions for corrections to be made.

provisions.title: Types of veterinary facilities subject to inspection
provisions.content: The veterinary facilities that will be subject to inspection that encompasses all aspects of health care for small and large animals and shall includes— (a) Veterinary hospital; (b) Veterinary clinic; (c) Veterinary Centre; (d) Veterinary Ambulatory Services; (e) Quarantine or Holding ground facilities. Part III – Procedure and types of inspections

provisions.title: Regular and routine facility inspections
provisions.content: (1) The Inspector shall inspect each registered veterinary facility every year in order to:— (a) ensurer that every registered veterinary facility is operating according to the minimum standards promulgated by the Council; (b) assist veterinary facilities in achieving and maintaining minimum standards and encourage them to continuously improve quality of veterinary services. (2) While seizing and detaining any drug or equipment, the subject to the provisions of sub-regulation (1) inspectors shall be accompanied by Police Officer.

provisions.title: 9 Non-regular routine facility inspections
provisions.content: The Inspector shall from time to time make non-regular routine facility inspections. Part IV – Re-inspections

provisions.title: Facility practice deficiencies
provisions.content: (1) When the facility ’s Inspector determines that a registered facility needs to make corrections to comply with the minimum standards, the inspector has the authority from the Council to:— (a) request the registered veterinary facility to submit a written document plan of correction within 30 days and the completed document shall be submitted to the Inspector for onwards transmission to the Council through the practice and facility Monitoring Committee; (b) follow up to ensure that appropriate plan is completed within the specified time period and that the minimum standards requirements are maintained. (2) When the Inspector observe that compliance with the minimum standards requirement has not been met, the uncorrected deficiencies shall be included in the Facility practice deficiencies report and the following steps shall take into effect:— (a) a stop order shall be issued to suspend the certificate of registration in respect of that facility until the deficiencies have been corrected; (b) the owner or operator of the facility will apply to the Council for re-inspection upon satisfying himself that the minimum standard requirements have been met. (3) For very serious violations of standard, the Inspector shall submit the findings and recommendations to the Council:— (a) the re-inspections shall be carried out after major corrections have been made and the registered veterinarian shall request for evaluation of his facility from the Council; (b) for each mandatory re-inspections the Council shall charge a fee that it sees fit;

provisions.title: Renewal of facility
provisions.content: (1) To be eligible for the renewal of the facility , the renewal application form shall be completed and submitted on the due date for Renewal of facility renewal to the Council; (2) The Inspector may visit and inspect the, facility if it has maintained the minimum standard requirements and submit a report to the Council for its consideration; (3) Facilities that fail to comply with the renewal rules or notification received by them concerning failure to timely renew or have their premises inspected on time appropriate action will be taken by the Council.

provisions.title: Reinstatement of registered facility
provisions.content: Reinstatement of a registered facility that have certification of registration expired beyond the post marked period, cancelled or suspended shall be accomplished upon meeting the following conditions:— (a) application shall be forwarded to the Council for reinstatement of the registration of the facility ; (b) compliance with inspection minimum standard requirements; (c) payment of all past due renewal and Council regulatory fees; and (d) payment of the late renewal fee as approved by the Council.

provisions.title: Voluntary inspection
provisions.content: Upon request, the Inspector may conduct inspection with a fee to assist a new facility or a new veterinarian setting up a veterinary hospital, veterinary clinic and/or veterinary centre.

provisions.title: The Council inspection compliance program
provisions.content: (1) The Council inspection compliance programme will be based on those minimum standards requirements found in the Registration of veterinary facilities rules, 2004 that are to be used in the care and treatment of animals and protect public health. (2) Any other area which is observed by the Inspector not meeting the statutory or regulatory requirements shall be covered during the inspection. (3) When conducting routine inspections on veterinary facilities, the Inspector shall use the minimum standards of the veterinary facilities and shall encompass the entire physical facility with particular attention but not limited to the following areas:— (a) structural; (b) personnel; (c) diagnostic facilities; (d) medical records; and (e) other general requirements as detailed under the registration of veterinary facilities rules. Part V – Frequency of inspections

provisions.title: Frequency of inspection
provisions.content: A routine inspection of each registered veterinary facility may be conducted every year.

provisions.title: New facility inspections
provisions.content: New facility inspections will be conducted within five days of the specific date requested by the applicant , provided the application is received within 30 days prior to the proposed opening date and after the initial inspection, new facilities will again be inspected within 12 months.

provisions.title: Reinspections
provisions.content: (1) Re-inspections will be conducted on those facilities receiving a compliance letter from the Council as requested. (2) The inspections will be conducted within 12 months of the date of the facility ’s response to the compliance letter and the Inspector shall wait 30-60 days ater the response is received to allow the facility time to demonstrate sustained compliance With the conditions stated in the compliance letter from the Council. (3) For facilities with deficiencies corrected and no new violation, the facility will be scheduled for an annual inspection. (4) For facilities with deficiencies not corrected or a new violation(s) noted, a report will be provided to the Council for appropriate action. Part VI – Veterinary facility costs

provisions.title: Inspection, registration, change of status and location and renewal fees
provisions.content: (1) The annual and renewal fees of inspections to the following veterinary facilities shall be as follows:— (a) Inspection fee (i) Veterinary Hospital 50,000/= (ii) Veterinary Clinic 30,000/= (iii) Veterinary Centre 20,000/= (iv) Veterinary Ambulatory services 20,000/= (v) Veterinary Laboratory 15,000/= (vi) Quarantine/Holding ground facilities 50,000/= (b) Initial Facility Registration (i) Veterinary Hospital 100,000/= (ii) Veterinary Clinic 50,000/= (iii) Veterinary Centre 20,000/= (iv) Quarantine facility 100,000/= (v) Veterinary Laboratory 30,000/= (vi) Veterinary Ambulatory service 50,000/= (c) Annual Retention fee (i) Veterinary Hospital 20,000/= (ii) Veterinary Clinic/Centre 10,000/= (iii) Quarantine facility 20,000/= (iv) Veterinary Laboratory 10,000/= (v) Veterinary Ambulatory service 20,000/= (d) Change of Facility Status 100,000/= (e) Location Chance of Facility 50,000/= (f) Late renewal: 3 months after expiry date (i) Veterinary Hospital 50,000/= (ii) Veterinary Clinic 30,000/= (iii) Veterinary Centre 15,000/= (iv) Quarantine Facility 50,000/= (v) Veterinary Laboratory 15,000/= (vi) Veterinary Ambulatory service 30,000/= (g) Late renewal: beyond 3 months to one year after expiry date: (i) Veterinary Hospital 75,000/= (ii) Veterinary Clinic 40,000/= (iii) Veterinary Centre 20,000/= (iv) Quarantine Facility 75,000/= (v) Veterinary Laboratory 20,000/= (vi) Veterinary Ambulatory service 40,000/= (2) The Council shall from time to time, review the rates of, fees fixed under these regulations. (3) The Names of veterinary facilities whose license renewals are more than 30 days over-due are brought to the next regular Council meeting and will be published in the Council’s newsletter or any other media as the Council may see it fit.
